description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.
The Coca-Cola Company is an American multinational corporation founded in January 1892 headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.

description: a purveyor of baby food and baby products
description_wiki: Gerber Products Company is an American purveyor of baby food and baby products headquartered in Fremont, Michigan.

surface_forms: California National Bank at 4 th and J Street

description: human settlement in Yolo County, California, United States of America
description_wiki: Broderick (formerly, Washington) is a former town in Yolo County, California, United States, now forming part of the City of West Sacramento.
Yolo County ( ; Wintun: Yo-loy), officially the County of Yolo, is a county located in the northern portion of the U.S. state of California.

description: county seat of Riverside County, California, United States
description_wiki: Riverside is a city in and the county seat of Riverside County, California, United States.
Sacramento Charter High School ("Sac High") is an independent public charter high school in the Oak Park neighborhood of Sacramento, California.

description: capital city of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima (広島市, Hiroshima-shi; English: , also UK: , US: , Japanese: [çiɾoɕima] ) is the capital of Hiroshima Prefecture in Japan.

description: largest city in the province of British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: Vancouver is a major city in Western Canada, located in the Lower Mainland region of British Columbia.

description: Japanese multinational banking and financial services company
description_wiki: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation (株式会社三井住友銀行, Kabushiki-gaisha Mitsui Sumitomo Ginkō; SMBC) is a Japanese multinational banking financial services institution owned by the Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, which is also known as the SMBC Group.
The Yokohama Specie Bank (横浜正金銀行, Yokohama Shōkin Ginkō; YSB) was a Japanese bank founded in Yokohama in 1880, which dominated the Japanese market for trade finance in subsequent decades.

description: American multinational banking and financial services corporation
description_wiki: The Bank of America Corporation (Bank of America; often abbreviated BAC or BofA) is an American multinational investment bank and financial services holding company headquartered at the Bank of America Corporate Center in Charlotte, North Carolina, with investment banking and auxiliary headquarters in Manhattan.
The Bank of Italy (Italian: Banca d'Italia, pronounced [ˈbaŋka diˈtaːlja], informally referred to as Bankitalia) is the national central bank for Italy within the Eurosystem.

description: commercial office complex in Calgary, Alberta, Canada
description_wiki: Southland Park is the name of a commercial office complex in Calgary, Alberta, Canada, comprising over 1,000,000 square feet (93,000 m2) of office space and consisting of four separate office buildings.

description: school that teaches the Japanese language to non-native speakers

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.
